title: Design a System — your taste, one client at a time
subtitle: A small starting shape for designers building a client design system while keeping their own taste-signature visible and evidenced across prior work.
scope: The designer's recurring taste across prior systems, plus the system being built for the current client engagement — alignment to the brief, derivation moves from prior systems, and the current state of the new system as pointers.
sourceCoverage: Template only; replace the example designer, prior systems, client brief, and primitives with your own.

templateMeta.useWhen: Use this when you are a designer building a system for one active client — and you also want to keep your own taste-signature visible and evidenced across the prior systems you have shipped.
goodFor: independent designers running one client engagement at a time
studios where one lead designer carries taste across systems
rebrands that derive heavily from prior work plus a new brief
design systems that need to track the brief-to-decision chain
templateMeta.operatingThesis: Tokens, components, and motifs live in Figma / tokens.json / a code repo. This doc surfaces them as pointers and records the thinking that produces them — what recurs across your prior work (taste-signature), what the brief asks for (alignment), and what you carry over from prior systems (derivation).
keepDistinct: derivation (kept / swapped / inverted / scaled / dropped from a prior system)
surface (current state of the primitives, as pointers)
notFor: settled architecture decisions with no live tension (use decision-record)
delivery sprints across a wider system (use capability-surface)

semanticDefinition.objectiveRole: Make design-system work legible by connecting taste evidence, brief alignment, derivation moves, and surface pointers.
semanticDefinition.templateTitle: Design a System — your taste, one client at a time
semanticDefinition.templateSubtitle: A small starting shape for designers building a client design system while keeping their own taste-signature visible and evidenced across prior work.
semanticDefinition.templateScope: The designer's recurring taste across prior systems, plus the system being built for the current client engagement — alignment to the brief, derivation moves from prior systems, and the current state of the new system as pointers.
semanticDefinition.templateObjective: Make the design system you are building visible end-to-end: where each primitive lives, why it answers the brief, what it inherits from your prior work, and what about it is uniquely you.
semanticDefinition.templateSuccessCondition: Every primitive in the surface has a pointer (not a duplicated value). Every alignment card has both a constraint and a response. Every derivation card has a prior-system pointer and a one-or-two-sentence rationale. Every recurring taste card has at least two prior-system pointers as evidence.

sections.title: Status Snapshot
sections.role: Summarize design-system readiness without carrying the design evidence.
sections.rationale: This section keeps client-system status visible while taste, alignment, derivation, and surface pointers stay distinct.

sections.title: Taste Signature
sections.role: Expose recurring designer traits evidenced by prior systems.
sections.rationale: Taste has to be evidenced through prior systems so it becomes an inspectable design input rather than a vague personal claim.

sections.title: Brief to System Alignment
sections.role: Translate client constraints into concrete system responses.
sections.rationale: Each alignment card pairs a brief constraint with a design response so the client system can be judged against the actual engagement.

sections.title: Design System Derivation
sections.role: Track what the new system inherits or transforms from prior work.
sections.rationale: Derivation cards keep inherited taste moves explicit and grounded in prior-system references.

sections.title: Design System Surface
sections.role: Expose the current design system as pointers to tokens, components, and motifs.
sections.rationale: The system surface should point to primitives where they live instead of duplicating token or component values inside the doc.

relationships.rationale: Design-system status should summarize the actual primitive surface being built.
Derivation moves should be grounded in evidenced taste from prior systems.

evidence.description: A derivation card should identify prior systems, tokens, components, or motifs that evidence the taste move.

relationships.rationale: System primitives should be traceable to brief constraints and responses.
Derivation moves become useful when they point at actual tokens, components, or motifs in the system surface.

stageSignals.question: What first token, component, or motif pointer makes this client system tangible?

stageSignals.when: taste-signature exists but design-system-derivation has no corresponding derivation moves
stageSignals.question: Which taste trait still needs a derivation move into the client system?

stageSignals.when: system surface exists but brief-to-system-alignment has no cards
stageSignals.question: Which brief constraint does the current primitive answer?

stageSignals.when: taste, alignment, derivation, and surface are all populated and linked
stageSignals.question: Can the system be defended as client-aligned, pointer-based, and distinctively grounded in prior taste?

validOperations.label: Add system surface primitive
validOperations.description: Add a token, component, or motif pointer that belongs to the client system.

validOperations.label: Add taste trait
validOperations.description: Add a taste-signature trait evidenced by prior systems.

validOperations.label: Add brief alignment
validOperations.description: Add a constraint/response card that ties the system to the client brief.

validOperations.label: Add derivation card
validOperations.description: Add a derivation move from prior system evidence to a current primitive.

objective: Make the design system you are building visible end-to-end: where each primitive lives, why it answers the brief, what it inherits from your prior work, and what about it is uniquely you.
successCondition: Every primitive in the surface has a pointer (not a duplicated value). Every alignment card has both a constraint and a response. Every derivation card has a prior-system pointer and a one-or-two-sentence rationale. Every recurring taste card has at least two prior-system pointers as evidence.

callouts.title: How to use this starter
items: Replace the priorSystems list with your own shipped work — id, name, url, year. Two or three is enough; this is evidence, not a portfolio.
Replace the clientBriefs list with the brief you are working against right now. One brief per active engagement.
Pointers, not copies. Every primitive in the surface section references where it actually lives — Figma variable, tokens.json key, repo path.
Taste needs evidence. A recurring trait means at least two prior-system pointers. One pointer is emerging.

callouts.title: Inspired by
items: The compositor architecture: entities have identity, scopes borrow properties from sources. Tokens live in Figma; this doc surfaces them.
Brad Frost, Atomic Design (2016) — for the primitive lifecycle vocabulary, adapted to a pointer-first model.

sections.title: Status Snapshot

callout.title: Reading order
items: Start with Taste Signature — what recurs across your work. That is the throughline before any client.
Then Brief to System Alignment — how this client's constraints map to design moves.
Then Derivation — what you carried over from prior systems and why.
End with the Surface — current state of the primitives as pointers.

sections.rationale: This section keeps client-system status visible while taste, alignment, derivation, and surface pointers stay distinct.

sections.title: Taste Signature

callout.title: How to read this section
items: Each card is one recurring trait, evidenced by prior systems where it appears.
Recurring needs at least two prior-system pointers. One pointer is still emerging.

data.name: Warm grays over neutral grays
data.trait: Greys carry a slight warm hue (yellow / red shift) over true neutral or cool greys, even when the client brand pulls cool.

evidence.text: Three of three recent systems. Atelier Norra had a cool brand mark but the supporting greys still leaned warm — the warmth survived the brief.

notes: Useful to call out early in a new engagement so warm-cool tension surfaces in the brief, not in the second round of comps.

data.name: Soft radius with low-elevation shadow
data.trait: Components default to a small radius (6–10px) with a low-elevation soft shadow. Reads as restraint, not flourish.

evidence.text: Same motif across all three; the radius value drifts (6, 8, 10) but the relationship — soft, low — is constant.

data.name: Generous spacing scales
data.trait: Spacing scale runs ~1.5x default (24px baseline rather than 16px). Whitespace reads as quality.

evidence.text: Studio Lin and Atelier Norra both used 24px baselines. Kraft used 20px under client pressure for density; that one is a partial counter-example.

notes: If a brief asks for density, this is the trait to negotiate first. Compressing later is cheap; expanding later is jarring.

data.name: Label-above-input form pattern
data.trait: Form labels sit above their inputs (not floating, not inline). Long-form readability over compact density.

evidence.text: Only one prior system — Kraft. Still emerging; would be recurring if Alto Audio also adopts it.

notes: Promote to recurring when a second prior-system pointer exists, not before.

sections.rationale: Taste has to be evidenced through prior systems so it becomes an inspectable design input rather than a vague personal claim.

sections.title: Brief to System Alignment

callout.title: How to read this section
items: Each card is one paired move: a constraint from the brief on one side, a design response on the other.
partial and conflicting are useful states. Do not paper over a tension that has not been resolved.

data.name: Premium feel ↔ generous spacing
data.constraint: Brief: "understated, premium feel — no neon, no gimmick".
data.response: Spacing scale set to 1.5x default; 24px baseline; padding tokens biased generous.

rationale.text: Generous whitespace reads as restraint. Compressing later is cheap; expanding later is jarring. Also matches a recurring trait — low risk for this designer.

data.name: No neon ↔ chroma cap on the palette
data.response: All hues in the brand and supporting palette capped at chroma 60 in OKLCH. Brand primary lives at chroma 48.

rationale.text: Chroma cap is the operational form of "no neon". Avoids relying on subjective designer call sheet by sheet.

data.name: Exceptionally accessible ↔ AAA contrast across body text
data.constraint: Brief: "exceptionally accessible — must outclass a typical consumer site".
data.response: All body text WCAG AAA against canvas; focus rings 3px and always visible, never opacity-faded on hover.

rationale.text: Body text and primary CTAs are AAA. Focus ring tokens drafted but not implemented in code yet — partial until coded.

data.name: Audiophiles AND musicians ↔ density toggle
data.response: Component density toggle (compact / comfortable) covering tables and lists. Shipping comfortable as default; compact deferred to v2.

rationale.text: Two audiences want different densities. v1 ships one, v2 adds the toggle once the comfortable-default ships and we can measure complaint patterns.

sections.rationale: Each alignment card pairs a brief constraint with a design response so the client system can be judged against the actual engagement.

sections.title: Design System Derivation

callout.title: How to read this section
items: Each card is one derivation move from a prior system, with rationale.
kept moves still need rationale — kept without rationale loses the data the derivation was built on.

data.name: Kept type scale from Studio Lin

rationale.text: 1.25 modular scale; works across marketing and product surfaces. No reason in this brief to redesign it.

data.name: Kept warm-gray base from Atelier Norra

rationale.text: Validated under a cool-leaning brand mark; survived the cool/warm tension in 2022. Same constraint here, same response.
Brief asks for premium softness; 8px reads as a bit 'studio-y'. 12px keeps the soft-radius motif but pushes it premium-ward. The soft-radius taste trait still recurs; only the magnitude moved.

data.name: Inverted: dark mode is primary, not optional

rationale.text: Kraft was light-default with a dark variant. Audiophile and studio contexts are dark-default; this brief flips the assumption. Light variant still ships, but dark is the canonical.
1px hairline borders at low contrast do not survive WCAG AAA in dark mode. The accessibility constraint outweighs the aesthetic carry-over. Dropping cleanly rather than half-keeping.

sections.rationale: Derivation cards keep inherited taste moves explicit and grounded in prior-system references.

sections.title: Design System Surface

callout.title: How to read this section
items: Each card is one primitive (token, component, or motif). The card never owns the value — it points at where it lives.
Status reflects the design-primitive lifecycle: proposed → in-figma → coded → shipped, with deprecated as the exit.

data.name: color/brand/primary
notes: OKLCH chroma 48; satisfies the 'no neon' alignment. Awaiting code import.

data.name: color/surface/canvas
notes: Carries the warm-gray base from Atelier; validated against AAA contrast for body text.

data.name: type/family/display
notes: Display face for headings only. Body family lives in type/family/text.

data.name: button/primary
notes: AAA contrast met in both light and dark modes; focus ring still pending implementation.

data.name: card/elevated
notes: Uses the swapped 12px radius motif, not the prior 8px.

data.name: 12px radius + soft shadow
notes: Carries the soft-radius taste trait at the new magnitude. Code implementation lands when the shadow tokens import.

data.name: focus/ring
notes: 3px outset, always visible. Currently the 'partial' on the accessibility-aaa alignment — promote to in-figma when drawn, coded when implemented.

sections.rationale: The system surface should point to primitives where they live instead of duplicating token or component values inside the doc.
